workstreams:

    name: SDK Installation & Core Configuration
    description: >
      Install @sentry/nextjs, create client/server config files,
      instrumentation hook, and wrap next.config.ts with withSentryConfig.
      Configure release tagging, environment detection, and explicitly
      disable tracing, session replay, and performance monitoring per FIB exclusions.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to:
      - "CAP:capture_server_runtime_exception"
      - "CAP:capture_error_boundary_crash"

      - "RULE-1"
      - "RULE-5"

    outputs:
      - sentry.client.config.ts

    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS2:
    name: PII Redaction Layer
    description: >
      Create beforeSend hook that scrubs casino-sensitive and player PII
      fields from error event payloads before transmission to Sentry.
      Denylist covers player identity (first_name, last_name, email, birth_date, etc.),
      financial metrics (buy_in, cash_out, win_loss, average_bet, balances),
      staff identifiers (*_by_staff_id variants), and recipient_email.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to:
      - "CAP:sanitize_error_payload"
      - "OUT-3"
      - "RULE-2"
    depends_on: [WS1]
    outputs:
      - lib/sentry/pii-denylist.ts
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS3:
    name: Error Boundary Integration
    description: >
      Wire Sentry.captureException into existing error boundary surfaces:
      global-error.tsx (replace placeholder comment) and
      PanelErrorBoundary componentDidCatch. Minimal 2-line edits per file.
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to:
      - "CAP:capture_error_boundary_crash"
      - "OUT-1"
      - "STEP-2"
      - "STEP-3"
    depends_on: [WS1]
    outputs:

      appear in a test error capture.

# Open-Question Resolutions
decisions:
  - decision_id: DEC-001
    resolves_open_question: "Exact SDK package choice"
    decision_statement: "@sentry/nextjs is the SDK — the official Sentry integration for Next.js App Router, handling both client and server instrumentation in a single package."
    rationale: "@sentry/nextjs is the only Sentry SDK that provides automatic Next.js App Router integration including instrumentation.ts hooks, withSentryConfig for source maps, and error boundary capture. No alternative SDK covers this surface."
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: assumption

  - decision_id: DEC-002
    resolves_open_question: "Exact alert sink"
    decision_statement: "Sentry built-in email alerts to project members, triggered on first occurrence of new issues. Single approved route per RULE-3."
    rationale: "Sentry's native alerting provides email notifications without requiring external integrations (Slack, PagerDuty) which are explicitly excluded. First-occurrence alerts keep noise low (OUT-4) while surfacing high-signal failures (OUT-5)."
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: assumption

  - decision_id: DEC-003
    resolves_open_question: "Exact source map upload wiring"
    decision_statement: "withSentryConfig() wrapper in next.config.ts uploads source maps in the configured production build pipeline using SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN, SENTRY_ORG, and SENTRY_PROJECT environment variables. Vercel is the current intended deployment host."
    rationale: "This is the standard @sentry/nextjs integration path. Source maps are uploaded during the production build, enabling stack trace resolution in Sentry console (OUT-2). SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN is a build-time secret, not exposed to the client. The wiring is not exclusive to Vercel — any CI that sets these env vars will upload source maps."
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: assumption

  - decision_id: DEC-004
    resolves_open_question: "Exact PII denylist fields"
    decision_statement: >
      Denylist covers 4 categories derived from database.types.ts analysis:
      (1) Player identity: first_name, last_name, middle_name, full_name, email,
      phone_number, birth_date, patron_first_name, patron_last_name,
      patron_date_of_birth, recipient_email;
      (2) Financial: average_bet, buy_in, cash_out, win_loss, ledger_balance,
      current_balance, balance_before, balance_after, final_average_bet,
      win_loss_cents, win_loss_estimated_total_cents, win_loss_inventory_total_cents,
      cash_out_observed_cents;
      (3) Staff identifiers: staff_id plus all *_by_staff_id variants
      (created_by, issued_by, replaced_by, voided_by, activated_by, closed_by,
      opened_by, paused_by, resumed_by, rolled_over_by, rundown_started_by);
      (4) Casino context: casino_id (correlatable to player records).
    rationale: "Fields identified by scanning types/database.types.ts and service DTOs. Categories align with FIB RULE-2 requirement to redact casino-, player-, and transaction-adjacent data."
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: cited

risks:
  - risk: "DEC-001 assumes @sentry/nextjs API is stable for current Next.js version"
    mitigation: "Verify compatibility during WS1 installation; consult Sentry docs for current Next.js App Router support"
  - risk: "CAP:route_high_signal_alert requires Sentry console configuration, not application code"
    mitigation: "Document as manual post-deployment step; alert configuration is in Sentry project settings, not in codebase"
  - risk: "Source map upload requires SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN in the production build pipeline"
    mitigation: "Add to build environment (currently Vercel) during WS1; source maps work without token in dev (local source maps)"

---

# EXECUTION-SPEC: FIB-H-SENTRY-001 - Pilot-bounded Sentry Error Tracking

## Overview

Add minimal, pilot-scoped Sentry error tracking to PT-2. This is an infrastructure integration that wires the Sentry SDK into existing error capture surfaces (server runtime, UI error boundaries) with a PII redaction layer to protect casino-sensitive data. No new user-facing surfaces, workflows, or bounded contexts are introduced.

## Scope

- **In Scope**: SDK installation, client/server config, PII scrubbing beforeSend hook, error boundary wiring (global-error.tsx, PanelErrorBoundary), environment variable setup, source map upload config, build verification
- **Out of Scope**: Distributed tracing, session replay, structured logging redesign, product analytics, custom dashboards, multi-channel alert routing, vendor abstraction layer, broad instrumentation

## Architecture Context

This is cross-cutting infrastructure — no bounded context ownership changes. The integration touches:
- **Build config**: next.config.ts (withSentryConfig wrapper)
- **Runtime hooks**: instrumentation.ts (Next.js instrumentation API)
- **Error surfaces**: Existing global-error.tsx and PanelErrorBoundary
- **Privacy layer**: New lib/sentry/ module for PII redaction

No SRM update required — Sentry is infrastructure, not a domain service.

## Workstream Details

### WS1: SDK Installation & Core Configuration

**Purpose**: Install @sentry/nextjs and create the foundational configuration files that enable error capture on both client and server runtimes.

**Deliverables**:
1. `sentry.client.config.ts` — Client-side Sentry.init with DSN, environment, release, sample rate 1.0, tracing disabled, replay disabled
2. `sentry.server.config.ts` — Server-side Sentry.init with same configuration
3. `instrumentation.ts` — Next.js instrumentation hook that imports server config
4. `next.config.ts` — Wrapped with withSentryConfig for source map upload
5. `@sentry/nextjs` added to package.json

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `npm run type-check` passes with new Sentry config files
- [ ] Sentry SDK is configured with `tracesSampleRate: 0` (RULE-5: no tracing)
- [ ] `replaysSessionSampleRate: 0` and `replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 0` (RULE-5: no replay)
- [ ] Environment is derived from `NEXT_PUBLIC_VERCEL_ENV` or `NODE_ENV`
- [ ] Release is tagged with `VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA` or build-time value
- [ ] `withSentryConfig` includes `silent: true` for clean build output

### WS2: PII Redaction Layer

**Purpose**: Implement beforeSend hook that removes casino-sensitive and player PII from error event payloads before they leave the application.

**Deliverables**:
1. `lib/sentry/pii-denylist.ts` — Exported denylist array and beforeSend function that recursively scrubs matching keys from event extras, contexts, and breadcrumb data

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] beforeSend hook is registered in both client and server configs
- [ ] All DEC-004 denylist fields are scrubbed from event payloads (RULE-2)
- [ ] Scrubbing is recursive — handles nested objects in extras/contexts
- [ ] Scrubbed values are replaced with `[Filtered]`, not deleted (preserves structure for debugging)
- [ ] Integration prefers minimal capture of extras/contexts/tags and uses denylist scrubbing as a secondary guard, not the sole privacy control
- [ ] `npm run type-check` passes

### WS3: Error Boundary Integration

**Purpose**: Wire Sentry.captureException into the two existing error boundary surfaces so unhandled exceptions are automatically reported.

**Deliverables**:
1. `app/global-error.tsx` — Add `Sentry.captureException(error)` in useEffect (replace placeholder comment at line 13)
2. `components/error-boundary/panel-error-boundary.tsx` — Add `Sentry.captureException(error)` in componentDidCatch (after existing logError call at line 103)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] global-error.tsx calls Sentry.captureException for all environments (not just dev)
- [ ] PanelErrorBoundary.componentDidCatch calls Sentry.captureException alongside existing logError
- [ ] No changes to error UI rendering or recovery behavior (RULE-4)
- [ ] `npm run lint` passes

### WS4: Validation & Build Verification

**Purpose**: Verify the complete integration doesn't break the application, meets quality gates, and PII redaction works end-to-end.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `npm run type-check` exits 0
- [ ] `npm run lint` exits 0
- [ ] `npm run build` exits 0 (production build with Sentry config)
- [ ] No `console.*` statements in new production code
- [ ] Synthetic server-side test error with seeded denylisted fields confirms scrubbed payload behavior
- [ ] Synthetic client-side error-boundary capture confirms scrubbed payload behavior
- [ ] At least one test event appears in Sentry with usable stack trace and release context

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Scope | Purpose |
|----------|-------|---------|
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN` | Client (primary), Server (fallback) | Sentry project DSN (public, safe for client bundle; server init uses only as fallback when SENTRY_DSN is absent) |
| `SENTRY_DSN` | Server only | Server-side DSN (optional, falls back to NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN) |
| `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` | Build only | Auth token for source map upload (secret) |
| `SENTRY_ORG` | Build only | Sentry organization slug |
| `SENTRY_PROJECT` | Build only | Sentry project slug |

## Environment Variable Precedence Rules

- Client init uses `NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN`
- Server init uses `SENTRY_DSN ?? NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN`
- If neither DSN is present, Sentry remains disabled without failing the build

## Post-Deployment Manual Steps (Required for Operational Acceptance)

1. **Create Sentry project** for PT-2 (if not already created)
2. **Configure alert rule** (REQUIRED — OUT-5 is not satisfied without this): First occurrence of new issue → email notification to project members (DEC-002, satisfies CAP:route_high_signal_alert / OUT-5). This is an operational dependency, not a code deliverable.
3. **Add env vars** to Vercel project settings (SENTRY_DSN, SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN, SENTRY_ORG, SENTRY_PROJECT)
4. **Verify**: Trigger a test error in production → confirm it appears in Sentry console with stack trace and no PII

## Write-Path Classification

**None detected.** This feature ships zero writes (no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, no server actions, no form submissions). No E2E mandate applies.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] All workstreams complete (WS1-WS4)
- [ ] All gates pass (type-check, lint, build)
- [ ] No PII fields in Sentry event payloads (validated with seeded denylist test cases, both server and client runtimes)
- [ ] Production build succeeds with Sentry configuration
- [ ] At least one deployed test event is visible in Sentry with usable stack trace and release context
- [ ] Required alert rule is configured and verified in Sentry for production (OUT-5 operational acceptance)
